authority: This file is the sole authority for the universe-builder's numbers (GR-1). Prose cites it; prose never restates it. Only the keystone writes it.

objective.metric: words (historical; retained for the record, no longer an optimization target)
objective.direction: none — the universe layer is audited, not optimized
objective.gate: node universe/audit.mjs — EXHAUSTIVE and DETERMINISTIC. Every claim is checked, not sampled. Against an exhaustive check a mirage is impossible: there is nothing to tune to.
objective.hardConstraint: every claim carries pathway + tier + status + a resolving trace; MYTH never in a method section; the layer table covers every corpus; the anchor law holds on every declared pair
objective.whyNotAHarness: The Fiat-Shamir Gap earns its cost only where the claim space is too large to check. This map makes ~100 enumerable claims. Rounds u1 and u2 applied a sampled gate here and produced no evidence about the map at a cost of ~1.4M tokens. See chronicles/2026-07-10_u3_retirement.md.

baseline.note: Re-measured after the keystone (a) retracted the field-guide-toy claim, (b) restored the dropped `cast` layer that audit.mjs caught on its first run, and (c) reframed the map around the auditor. The map GREW. Growth is honest: the objective was to shrink a map under a sampled gate, and that objective is retired. See runs[].u3.

best.note: equal to baseline. No round ever validated anything. The metric is retained as a HISTORICAL number, not a live objective — the universe layer is now audited exhaustively, not optimized adversarially.

corpora.authority: papers/v6 for maths; the conjecture register for C-ids
the build; mirrors cite, never edit
the directory tree, not the stale MAPPING.md counts

runs.reason: session token limit killed 12 of 17 agents, including all four Gap draws. No witnesses derived; no gate ran.
runs.yield: a defect in engine/dual_agent_loop.mjs: dead seats were counted as dry rounds, so the loop reported an outage as exhaustion. Fixed; pinned by engine/loop.test.mjs.
runs.survivors: measure + 4 proposals, unassayed. Candidates, not results (GR-5). Cached against the run for replay.

runs.reason: 9/9 agents, zero errors. Both seeds re-derived by the auditor's command; both verdicts sound.
runs.note: MIS-GATED. Both candidates BEAT the metric (7514, 7732 < 7753) and both scored 0/8, because the Gap drew arbitrary source facts (a licence string, a JSON version field, a code expression) from 612 KB of sources that the 7 KB map was never responsible for carrying. The feasible set was empty: objective.metric says compress, the gate demanded a transcript. A gate no candidate can pass is a wall, and the verdicts measure the config, not the map.
runs.yield: the critic's taxonomy had no word for a bad gate — structural/probe-limited/noise all blame the proposer — so it convicted the proposer on sound evidence. Added the 'mis-gated' classification (seats/critic.md). Added the mustAnswer scope contract per target. Corrected universe/README.md's false claim that this instance is the field-guide toy promoted: the toy's candidate is a compression OF its source; this map is 1.1% of its sources.
runs.killedLeversFiled: NONE, deliberately. A kill recorded against a gate the lever could not pass is a fence around good ground (GR-6). Both levers remain unjudged.
runs.fairCatch: one drawn fact was legitimate and survives: the map asserted the substrate coherence audit as PROVEN without stating its re-runnable command, which ADMISSION §2 requires. The tier-dependent probe tests the map's own claims and was always sound.
runs.unclaimedFinding: the by-container lens observed the map describes FIVE layers while frontier.json enumerates SIX corpora (skills → cast is dropped) and conformChecks refuses fewer than six. Produced by a round that scored zero; not folded, because no gate validated it.

runs.reason: No round was run. The keystone retired the universe-builder harness instead, on the argument that an exhaustive auditor strictly dominates a sampled gate when every claim is enumerable.
runs.yield: universe/audit.mjs — exhaustive, deterministic, zero agents. On its FIRST run it caught the dropped `cast` layer: the defect the u2 by-container lens had found but could not validate, because its round scored 0/8 against an impossible gate. Milliseconds and zero tokens, against 811k tokens and no verdict.

openItems: objective.canary is proposed for the core SEAT_CONTRACT (a reference artifact the gate must pass by construction). conform.mjs WARNS when absent; it does not yet FAIL, because making it fail would break sibling instances mid-flight. Flipping it to a hard failure is a First-Person call.

The retired loop lives in universe/retired/. It is prior art and a worked negative result, not dead weight — HARNESS_PATHS.md cites it as the domain where the harness was applied and should not have been.
FLEET.md section 7 names a First-Person decision (co-present rite vs key custody); SEATS.md section 5 argues RPP already settles it. Neither is a verdict.
ERRATA E-1..E-8 are recorded, not fixed. Each belongs to the corpus that owns it.
